author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: Agreed. It always included passive and active. I'm not involved with that discussion. One observation, I think it is dangerous to keep referring to $96 per licence per month. The indicative  19,000 per month per Uno is a total across 200 licences and I think referring to the average is going to cause problems later on, particularly if this makes its way into Uno marketing. Core of my question is about clarity so that we are not having to speculate. Have a great day!

author: BeamNode
msg:
#ama

Has the Unity team already looked into possible ways in which individuals or coordinated groups could use the system to their advantage to extract excessive payments, especially with the new third party task ‘scout and runner’?

Can the Unity payment system for this task be compromised if participants intentionally or accidentally perform incorrect actions? Is it possible for individuals or organized groups to exploit weaknesses in the system to maximize payouts?

As the network grows, could people eventually find ways to exploit the system to extract excessive payments, potentially harming its long term sustainability, and are there systems in place or planned to monitor the network and detect such behavior, including coordinated abuse by groups of participants?

author: Calus B
msg: Well couple things that stood out here. They know exactly what they are building and how to utilize it. They have a pretty large backlog of jobs that they have already finished the work on converting them to Unity and know they work. They have a much larger pool of jobs that they’ve started with the high-level conceptual idea about how it would work on unity. They are working on adding more people to this to help with the load of adding all these Jobs.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: Any sense of time scales?

author: Calus B
msg: I think the alpha will start on scout and runner this month. Probably the next two weeks.  in parallel They are rolling out other jobs as well.  They did not tell us how many but they did say every day there’s a noticeable expansion of the network and people are still picking up licenses.

author: Calus B
msg: What was interesting was how much they actually have planned for unity as a network

author: Calus B
msg: That vision, of course, would be over the next four or five years, but they’re looking at this as a resource to be expanded and I think that will serve note holders incredibly well

author: Opher
msg: I see how the ENO’s have scheduled meetings. I think it’d be smart to do the same with UNO’s. I mean WE are the reason the network is UP and RUNNING. The X Spaces feel more for the wide audience and not as in depth with not as much context that you would expect to people who paid large sums to be involved, not just leasing a license off the marketplace at no cost. I personally want to know more how this all runs and get legit in depth answers on the best way to optimize our network configurations and best practices to maximize uptime and reward efficiency. I want to  be more involved. I don’t have the social profile built to be an ambassador to get access to more details. But again I feel as if we should have actual UNO calls. I hope I’m conveying this correctly without coming across wrong haha.

Not sure when will they decide to go ahead with monthly fees but for me the absolute base line is to have double the earning after 1.99. So you esrn 6usd, 2 usd is fee (still not really really sure what these fees actually are to cover really but u take it at face value). And 4 usd us left to earn and solit based on license setup. 
I reckon UNO will mostly pay the fee initially can’t imagine friends being forced to pay something initially when it earns literally one beer per month (yes in my country goood draft beer costs 2 usd).

author: Calus B
msg:
[Photo] **🤝**** Unity - New Web Management Capabilities Now Live
**
Unity is expanding its web-based management tools for both Unity Node Operators (UNOs) and Unity License Operators (ULOs), with new capabilities now live - and more on the way.

UNO and ULO network incentives can now be withdrawn directly through the Unity Web Management Portal.
Simply visit https://manage.unitynodes.io, sign in with your account, and you can securely access and withdraw your rewards from anywhere.

The next major update will introduce full web-based UNO and ULO License management. This will allow operators to organise, monitor, and manage all licenses through any modern browser - on desktop, laptop, or mobile web.

These upgrades significantly expand platform compatibility, improve global accessibility, strengthen compliance, and ensure Unity can be used seamlessly across all device types.

More web-based features will continue rolling out as the platform evolves.

➡️ Visit unitynodes.io to learn more and secure your Unity Node Software License

🔗 https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/1994870014512386187

🤝  **Official Unity** **Resources**
| [**Unity**](https://unitynodes.io/) | [**Unity Litepaper**](https://unitynodes.io/documents/litepaper.pdf) | [**QR Code Login**](https://uno.unitynodes.io/) | [**Manage**](https://manage.unitynodes.io/) | [**Telegram**](https://t.me/unitynodes) | [**X**](https://x.com/UnityNodesIO) | [**YouTube**](https://youtube.com/@UnityNodesIO)

[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - iOS App Now Live on the Apple App Store**

author: Quoia75
msg:
I keep seeing this repeated like hopium. I think it's partially / fractionally true, but Josh was clear in a past AMA: UNOs are expected to do some work to attract ULOs.

Problem is - if I pay to attract ULOs, I need a way for these people to pick up and retain **MY** licenses. So far as I know no features exist that will allow us to do that.
